children.value: This example demonstrates a complete external aerodynamics workflow — meshing and simulating airflow around a car to calculate drag and lift coefficients and visualize the flow field.
Generate a mesh around a car geometry with boundary layers
Run a RANS simulation at highway speed (30 m/s / ~110 km/h)
Calculate drag coefficient (Cd) and lift coefficient (Cl)
Visualize pressure distribution, wake structure, and streamlines
Upload your car geometry file (STEP format recommended)
If your car model has open wheel arches or an open underbody, the mesher will struggle. Ensure the geometry is a closed, solid body. The AI Assistant can help identify and fix issues.
— the car should sit on a flat surface
Allow flow to develop before reaching the car
Allow flow over the roof to develop
Add refinement zones to capture key flow features:
Behind the car, extending 2× car length downstream
Under the car, from bumper to bumper
and wait for completion. Typical cell counts:
Select the mesh from your meshing project
ground must be a moving wall

at the same speed as the freestream. This simulates the car moving through still air, rather than air blowing over a stationary car on a stationary floor (which would create an unrealistic ground boundary layer).
— look for all residuals dropping below 1e-4
A typical vehicle RANS simulation converges in 300–800 iterations.
Reference area: Frontal area of the car (approximately width × height, e.g., 2.2 m²)
(blue) on the roof and sides where flow accelerates
at Y = 0 (centerline) colored by velocity magnitude
The wake behind the car appears as a low-velocity region
seeded from the inlet to see flow paths around the car
Color by velocity to see flow acceleration under the car
You can set up this entire example through conversation:
“I’ve uploaded a sedan CAD file. Set up an external aero mesh for highway speed analysis.”
Analyzes geometry dimensions, suggests domain box, recommends mesh settings, boundary layers, and refinement zones. Presents configuration for your approval.
“Run a simulation at 30 m/s with k-omega SST”
Configures boundary conditions, sets inlet velocity, applies moving ground, and runs the simulation with your confirmation.
Calculates forces on the car body surface and reports Cd and Cl.
